subject: Physics
title: Light in a Vacuum
description: A short lesson to help you apply Light in a Vacuum.

hook.text: In empty space, light needs no medium to travel. It carries its own electric and magnetic fields.

buildup.text: Sound needs air. Water waves need water. But light crosses the vacuum of space with nothing to vibrate.

discovery.text: Maxwell's equations showed light is self-sustaining—a changing electric field creates a magnetic one and vice versa.

twist.text: This self-propagation means light from the Big Bang is still traveling today, 13.8 billion years later.

climax.text: Sunlight crossing 150 million km of vacuum reaches your eyes in just 8 minutes and 20 seconds.

punchline.text: No medium needed. Light carries itself across the universe.

quiz.question: Why can light travel through a vacuum?
options: Its changing electric and magnetic fields sustain each other
It bounces off invisible particles in space
